feedback.details: Diagram validates successfully with mmdc (exit code 0). All 8 relationships (lines 16-23) render without errors. No syntax issues detected.

Diagram contains exactly: 1 System box (llm-switch, line 8), 2 Person_Ext boxes (Developers line 6, Operations Engineers line 7), and exactly 6 System_Ext boxes (Nomad line 9, Consul line 10, Vault line 11, Local Model Servers line 12, Frontier API Providers line 13, Observability Tools line 14). No Container or Component boxes present.

All 6 external systems have labeled relationships with protocols: nomad ('Nomad job specification', line 18), consul ('HTTP/DNS', line 19), vault ('HTTP/TLS', line 20), localmodels ('HTTP/gRPC', line 21), frontierapis ('HTTP', line 22), observability ('HTTP/gRPC', line 23). User relationships also labeled with protocols.

File contains exactly four H2 sections with adequate word counts: 'System Context' (line 28, ~256 words, threshold 150 met), 'User Roles' (line 38, ~133 words, threshold 100 met), 'External Dependencies' (line 43, ~180 words, threshold 100 met), and 'Key Interactions' (line 55, ~170 words, threshold 150 met). Total 1145 words.

All diagram elements include PRD references (lines 6-23). All narrative paragraphs end with PRD references (e.g., [PRD-Executive Summary] line 29, [PRD-FR1, FR2] line 56). Minor deduction: some references use descriptive section names (e.g., 'PRD-Domain-Specific Requirements - Infrastructure Reliability & Performance') rather than specific feature requirement numbers.

All 7 required technologies explicitly listed: Golang ('Go service' line 32), Docker ('Docker containers' line 32), Nomad (lines 32, 46, 57), Consul (lines 32, 46, 58), Vault (lines 32, 46, 58), vLLM (lines 12, 33, 36), and Langfuse (lines 34, 39). Diagram uses correct C4 shapes (hexagon for System, Person_Ext for users, System_Ext rectangles for external systems).

All 5 edge cases explicitly documented: (1) Zero-code-change: line 41 'zero-code-change integration pattern'. (2) Multiple model types: lines 12-13 distinction between local models and frontier APIs. (3) Nomad deployment constraints: line 57 'placement constraints favoring GPU-equipped nodes'. (4) API timeout scenarios: lines 35, 53 mention configurable timeouts and circuit breaker patterns. (5) Network partition tolerance: lines 36, 52, 63 address degraded mode operation when Consul/Vault unavailable.

File begins with H1 '# C1 System Context' (line 1), followed immediately by Mermaid diagram block (lines 3-26), followed by narrative sections (line 28+). No extraneous text between H1 and diagram. Correct ordering maintained.

Case-sensitive label mismatch: Diagram uses 'Nomad Cluster' (line 9) while narrative uses 'Nomad' (lines 44, 46, 57) and 'Nomad cluster' (line 40). Per contract: 'All labels in the Mermaid diagram match exactly (case-sensitive) with terms used in the narrative section.' This is the SAME issue noted in Round 4 that persists. All other labels match exactly: 'Developers' (lines 6, 39), 'Operations Engineers' (lines 7, 40), 'llm-switch' (lines 8, 29), 'Local Model Servers' (lines 12, 48), 'Frontier API Providers' (lines 13, 49), 'Observability Tools' (lines 14, 50).

overall_summary: The C1 System Context document is production-ready with comprehensive coverage of all criteria except one recurring issue. The Mermaid diagram validates successfully, implements C4 Level 1 correctly (1 system, 2 people, 6 external systems), and all relationships are labeled with protocols. The narrative meets all word count requirements across four H2 sections. PRD traceability is excellent with references throughout. Technology stack compliance is complete with all 7 required technologies mentioned. Edge case coverage is thorough, addressing zero-code-change integration, multiple model types, Nomad constraints, API timeouts, and network partitions. The ONLY outstanding issue is a recurring Label Consistency failure (Round 4 noted this, persists in Round 5): 'Nomad Cluster' in diagram (line 9) vs 'Nomad' in narrative (lines 44, 46, 57). This case-sensitive mismatch violates the 9.0 threshold. Fix: change line 9 to either 'Nomad' to match narrative, or update narrative to consistently use 'Nomad Cluster'.
